title: Incorrect results with `GROUP BY WITH ROLLUP` and `uniqExact` under specific column values
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
Using `uniqExact` with `GROUP BY WITH ROLLUP` sometimes produces incorrect results (occasionally “blinking” between the correct and incorrect value). The incorrect value always matches the summary row.
Observations:
- Modifying the column used for grouping (either in SQL, e.g. +1 or `toString`, or before inserting data into the database) makes the bug disappear (at least on the sample dataset).
- Replacing `uniqExact` with `uniq` or removing `WITH ROLLUP` also makes the bug disappear.
- Between versions (from 25.6 to 25.8) the behavior varied slightly (e.g. in one version the results were “blinking,” while in another they were consistently incorrect).
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.6.1+ (I've checked every minor version from 25.1 to 25.8)
### How to reproduce
**Version:** v25.8.2.29
**Schema**:
```
CREATE DATABASE reproduction
ENGINE = Atomic;
CREATE TABLE reproduction.tree_nodes
(
`date` Date,
`c1` LowCardinality(String),
`c2` UInt32,
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(date)
ORDER BY (date, c1, c2)
SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192;
CREATE TABLE reproduction.hits
(
`date` Date,
`c1` LowCardinality(String),
`c2` Nullable(UInt32),
`c3` UInt32,
`c4` UInt32,
)
ENGINE = MergeTree
PARTITION BY toYYYYMM(date)
ORDER BY (date, c1, c3)
SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192;
```
**Sample data**
[tree_nodes.csv.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22254670/reproduction.factorized.tree_nodes.csv.gz)
[hits.csv.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/22254669/reproduction.factorized.hits.csv.gz)
**Query**
```
SELECT
nt,
uniqExact(filtered.c2) AS result
FROM
(
SELECT
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE reproduction_test.tree_nodes (date DATE, c1 VARCHAR, c2 INTEGER);
SELECT date, COUNT(DISTINCT c2) AS unique_count FROM reproduction_test.tree_nodes GROUP BY date UNION ALL SELECT NULL, COUNT(DISTINCT c2) FROM reproduction_test.tree_nodes;

SELECT date, COUNT(DISTINCT c2) AS unique_count FROM reproduction_test.tree_nodes WHERE c2 IS NOT NULL GROUP BY date UNION ALL SELECT NULL, COUNT(DISTINCT c2) FROM reproduction_test.tree_nodes WHERE c2 IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: Using uniqExact with GROUP BY WITH ROLLUP produces incorrect results
